description: Overhead crane load (2.5 MT pipe spool) swung unexpectedly during lifting due to improper rigging — missed worker by approximately 1 meter
root_cause: Rigger used single-point lift instead of required two-point lift for long spool; spool length 6m exceeded single-point lift limit of 3m
immediate_cause: Improper rigging technique; non-compliance with lifting plan
contributing_factors: Rigger not trained in new lifting procedure (updated Nov-2025); supervisor absent from lift area; no pre-lift toolbox talk conducted
corrective_action: Rigger retrained and re-certified; supervisor counseled; lifting plan compliance made mandatory checkpoint before any crane operation
preventive_action: Implement mandatory pre-lift checklist signed by rigger AND supervisor; install camera monitoring on all overhead cranes; monthly rigging audit
lessons_learned: Single-point vs two-point lift decision MUST be part of pre-lift toolbox talk; supervisor presence is non-negotiable for loads >1 MT

description: Technician received minor steam burn on left forearm while opening inspection port on economizer during running boiler inspection; steam leak from deteriorated gasket around port
injury_type: Minor burn — first degree; 5cm area on left forearm
root_cause: Inspection port gasket had deteriorated beyond acceptable condition; gasket replacement was overdue by 2 months per PM schedule
immediate_cause: Opening inspection port without verifying gasket condition; not wearing heat-resistant arm guards (required PPE for this task)
contributing_factors: PM schedule adherence gap; PPE compliance not checked before task start
corrective_action: Gasket replaced; all economizer inspection port gaskets inspected and replaced where needed; burn treated at plant first-aid center
preventive_action: Add gasket condition check to pre-task checklist for economizer inspection; enforce arm guard PPE through mandatory PPE checkpoint before entering boiler house
lessons_learned: Gasket condition is a leading indicator for steam leak risk — must be tracked in PM system with no tolerance for overdue replacement

description: Erection helper fell 3 meters from scaffold platform while positioning a structural column bracket; safety harness was worn but not hooked to anchor point. Sustained fracture of right wrist and bruised ribs.
root_cause: Worker unhooked harness lanyard to move to adjacent scaffold bay where no anchor point was available; anchor points not extended to cover new scaffold extension
immediate_cause: Working at height without fall protection connected; gap in anchor point coverage on extended scaffold
contributing_factors: Scaffold extension was added previous day without corresponding anchor point installation; site supervisor did not inspect scaffold before work commenced
corrective_action: Worker hospitalized and treated; scaffold inspected and anchor points installed at all bays; 100% scaffold inspection before work clearance implemented; L&T issued show-cause to their site supervisor
preventive_action: Implement scaffold handover checklist with mandatory anchor point verification; install retractable lifeline systems on all scaffolds >2m; daily pre-shift scaffold inspection by EHS
investigated_by: EHS Manager V. Desai + L&T EHS
lessons_learned: CRITICAL: Scaffold modifications MUST trigger re-inspection before any work clearance. Anchor point coverage must be 100% — zero tolerance for gaps.

description: Approximately 200 liters of phosphate-based boiler water treatment chemical leaked from a damaged IBC container due to forklift puncture during handling. Chemical entered floor drain before containment.
root_cause: Forklift tine punctured IBC wall during positioning in storage rack; forklift operator was using incorrect fork attachment for IBC handling
immediate_cause: Wrong fork attachment used; IBC placement too tight in rack allowing minimal clearance
contributing_factors: Storage area layout not reviewed after recent racking modification; forklift operators not briefed on clearance requirements for new rack configuration
corrective_action: Spill contained using absorbent materials; floor drain blocked to prevent environmental release; affected drain section flushed and neutralized; IBC replaced; area cleaned and tested
preventive_action: Install IBC-specific fork attachments on designated forklift; revise storage rack spacing for minimum 150mm side clearance; install secondary containment bunds around chemical storage; chemical spill drill quarterly
lessons_learned: Chemical spill response was delayed by 8 minutes because spill kit was located 50m away. Relocate spill kits to within 10m of all chemical storage areas.

description: Loaded truck entering plant hit security barrier arm while gate was being raised; barrier arm broke and struck the security guard cabin. Guard was inside cabin and was startled but uninjured.
root_cause: Truck driver accelerated before barrier was fully raised; driver was unfamiliar with plant entry procedure (first-time visitor)
immediate_cause: Driver impatience; no physical stop barrier before the automatic arm barrier
contributing_factors: Vehicle entry SOP does not include physical wheel stop; no speed breaker before barrier; guard relied on visual signal which driver missed
corrective_action: Barrier repaired; driver counseled; temporary manual barrier control implemented until speed management solution installed
preventive_action: Install speed breakers 20m before barrier; add traffic lights (red/green) visible to drivers; install wheel stops that lower only when barrier is fully up; add driver induction video at gate for first-time visitors
lessons_learned: Gate entry sequence needs engineering controls not just procedural controls. Human error at vehicle entry points is predictable and must be designed out.
